text:
There once was a wise old owl named Owly. Owly lived deep in an owl-friendly forest. Every morning he would glide through tall moss. You see, Owly loved wisdom. He knew a thing or two about it himself.

One day Owlry heard about a wise owl from somewhere distant. He decided to visit. When he arrived, this owl wore glasses rimmed with feathers.

"Why owl eyes!" Owly exclaimed.

"Eyes see all."

"How wise."

"A feather wears wisdom."

An owl listened. Together they pondered. By nightfall they knew wisdom wasn. Owly could teach.

They traveled afar.
